seo_description: Current time in Minsk, Belarus. Further-eastern European Time (FET, UTC+3) year-round — no DST. Live clock and time differences for Minsk.

faq.question: What time is it in Minsk right now?
faq.answer: Minsk, Belarus uses Further-eastern European Time (FET, UTC+3) year-round. Belarus does not observe Daylight Saving Time since 2011. The IANA identifier is Europe/Minsk. Minsk is 1 hour ahead of Moscow (despite their geographic proximity) and 3 hours ahead of London. The live clock above shows the current Minsk time.

faq.question: What time zone is Minsk in?
faq.answer: Minsk, Belarus uses Further-eastern European Time (FET, UTC+3) year-round. The IANA identifier is Europe/Minsk. Belarus abolished Daylight Saving Time in 2011 — the country was supposed to join Russia (also UTC+3) permanently, and did so that year. Minsk shares UTC+3 with Moscow, Riyadh, Istanbul, and Nairobi.

faq.question: Does Minsk observe daylight saving time?
faq.answer: No. Minsk, Belarus abolished Daylight Saving Time in 2011 and now uses UTC+3 (FET) permanently year-round. This means that in summer, Moscow and Minsk are on the same time, but in winter, Moscow (MSK, UTC+3) also stays at UTC+3 — keeping the two capitals at the same offset permanently.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Minsk and London?
faq.answer: Minsk (FET, UTC+3) is 3 hours ahead of London (GMT, UTC+0) in winter. During UK summer time (BST, UTC+1), Minsk is 2 hours ahead. Belarus does not observe DST.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Minsk and New York?
faq.answer: Minsk (FET, UTC+3) is 8 hours ahead of New York (EST, UTC-5) in winter. During US DST (EDT, UTC-4), Minsk is 7 hours ahead. Belarus does not observe DST.

faq.question: Is Minsk in the same time zone as Moscow?
faq.answer: Yes. Minsk (FET, UTC+3) and Moscow (MSK, UTC+3) are in the same UTC offset. Both countries abolished DST — Russia in 2014, Belarus in 2011 — and both settled on UTC+3 year-round. When it is noon in Moscow, it is noon in Minsk.
